summary: Full end-to-end testing of the two new features: (1) SCHEDULE SNAPSHOTS (Duplica Settimana) backend + frontend, and (2) NOTIFICATION SOUNDS + Avvisi tab BADGE. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests passed (auth gating on all 4 endpoints for anon + non-admin client, CRUD create/list/delete with default & custom name, list projection excludes 'lessons' field + descending order, restore E2E: 11 lessons -> delete 1 -> restore -> verify 11 lessons back with all (giorno/orario/tipo_attivita/coach) keys preserved, 400 bad id, 404 not found). Frontend: after supervisor restart the LessonScheduleManager rendered the third 'Duplica Settimana' button with testID schedule-manage-snapshots correctly; opened sub-modal with snapshot-input-name + snapshot-create-btn; created a snapshot from UI -> list refreshed with snapshot-row-*, snapshot-delete-*, snapshot-restore-* buttons visible. Notification sound: created a test announcement via API, then opened the app with empty localStorage -> popup with TEST_notif shown AND localStorage 'announce_sounds_played' populated with the announcement ID ["6a4cf1fa9762c90828f69244"]. Avvisi tab bottom-bar badge showed the numeric '1' as expected. Reloaded page a second time: popup shown again but 'announce_sounds_played' remained identical (ding NOT re-fired), as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py continues to grow (now ~7724 lines including new snapshot section + announcements). Splitting into router modules (admin_schedule_router, admin_announcements_router, etc.) is increasingly important for maintainability.

Snapshot lessons cap: create_schedule_snapshot uses db.lessons.find({}).to_list(200). If schedule ever grows beyond 200 lessons, snapshot will silently truncate. Consider explicit unbounded or a much higher cap with logging.
list_schedule_snapshots returns max 100 snapshots (to_list(100)). Since snapshots are user-managed and can accumulate over time, consider adding pagination or a soft cap warning in the UI.
The 'ding' sound relies on Web Audio API on web only (Platform.OS !== 'web' is a noop) — mobile native users won't hear it. Not blocking but worth documenting for Daniele.

AnnouncementColor validation is case-sensitive (only 'blue/green/orange/red/yellow' accepted, not 'blu'). The AdminAnnouncementPopup UI presumably already enforces this, but any external API consumer could easily 400 out.
seed_data_creation: During UI test created one snapshot 'TEST_ui_pw_final' and one announcement 'TEST_notif' -> both deleted at end. Pytest suite auto-cleans TEST_-prefixed snapshots via fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend service required a supervisor restart to pick up the latest LessonScheduleManager.tsx changes on first testing pass — data-testid schedule-manage-snapshots was initially absent from the DOM even though the source contained it. After 'sudo supervisorctl restart frontend' + waiting ~10s for Metro to re-bundle, everything rendered correctly. If a similar 'testid missing' anomaly appears in future iterations, restart frontend and retry before assuming a code bug. Also: the persistent admin popups '4 PROVE ATTIVATE' / '10 NUOVI ISCRITTI' can be bypassed by pressing Escape (not just clicking OK, VISTO! which re-triggers them). Announcements POST requires 'colore' in [blue|green|orange|red|yellow] (not italian 'blu').
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues. Both features are functional end-to-end.
